description: CodeBoarding is an AI‑powered static analysis platform that automatically generates architectural diagrams and health reports for a codebase. It orchestrates a multi‑stage pipeline: a CLI entry point discovers the project and sets up the environment, then a static analysis engine builds a detailed call graph via Language Server Protocol adapters. Incremental change detection maps Git diffs to the existing graph, allowing only modified sections to be re‑processed. An AI reasoning layer leverages LLMs to interpret the structural data and produce high‑level architectural descriptions. Finally, health metrics and visual documentation are rendered into formats such as Mermaid.js, HTML, and Markdown, with results persisted in a local DuckDB store.

components.name: Orchestration & Lifecycle
components.description: Serves as the system's entry point, managing CLI commands, project discovery, and the high‑level execution flow. It coordinates the initialization of language servers and the overall analysis pipeline.

components.name: CLI & Execution Orchestrator
components.description: The primary entry point that manages the high-level state machine of the application. it handles CLI parsing, validates arguments, and determines whether to process a local or remote repository.

components.name: CLI & Environment Controller
components.description: Responsible for the initial application bootstrap, including parsing CLI arguments, validating user configurations, and initializing the LLM provider infrastructure. It acts as the gateway that sets up the monitoring and tracing context before execution begins.

components.name: Execution State Orchestrator
components.description: The central state machine of the subsystem. it manages the high-level workflow for both local and remote (GitHub Action) analysis runs. It utilizes a RunContext to track execution state and coordinates the DiagramGenerator to perform the actual "smart" analysis.

components.name: Workspace & Repository Handler
components.description: Manages the physical code environment, including cloning remote repositories into temporary workspaces and applying ignore patterns (e.g., .gitignore) to filter the analysis scope. It provides the file-system abstraction layer for the orchestrator.

components.name: Analysis Schema & Persistence
components.description: Defines the structured data contracts for analysis results and handles the serialization of internal components into persistent JSON artifacts. It ensures that the output of the orchestrator is standardized for downstream generators.

components.name: Health & Quality Auditor
components.description: A specialized execution branch that performs static architectural health checks, such as identifying circular dependencies. It can be run as a standalone CLI command or integrated into the main analysis workflow.

components_relations.relation: Initiates the analysis workflow with validated arguments and initialized LLM providers.
Requests repository preparation and file filtering to define the analysis boundary.
Commits and serializes the final analysis results into the unified JSON format.
Triggers architectural health reports as a sub-step of the comprehensive repository analysis.
Contributes health-specific metadata to the final analysis data model.

components.name: Environment & Tooling Manager
components.description: Ensures the local machine has the necessary runtime dependencies (Node.js, NPM) and language-specific binaries (LSP servers) required for static analysis.

components.name: Installation Orchestrator
components.description: Acts as the central controller for the environment setup process. it determines whether the local environment satisfies requirements by checking tool fingerprints and triggers the appropriate installation workflows (Node or Native) when necessary.

components.name: Node.js & LSP Runtime Manager
components.description: Manages the Node.js ecosystem within the tool. It handles the bootstrapping of NPM, manages virtual Node environments, and installs NPM-based Language Server Protocol (LSP) servers like Pyright or TypeScript.

components.name: Native Tooling & Binary Provider
components.description: Responsible for the acquisition and management of non-Node dependencies. It identifies platform-specific asset URLs, downloads compressed archives, verifies binary integrity, and extracts them into the local tooling directory.

components.name: Environment Configuration & Registry
components.description: Provides the foundational data and utility layer for the subsystem. It resolves installation paths, loads user-specific tool configurations, and provides low-level repository management primitives (like git cloning) used during setup.

components.description: Translates discovered file types and project structures into concrete static analysis configurations, initializing Language Server Protocol (LSP) adapters for each identified language.

components.description: Identifies and parses project-level dependency files to provide LLM agents with ecosystem context and manages the metadata cache to support incremental analysis.

components.name: Agentic Analysis Pipeline
components.description: Coordinates the "Hybrid Analysis" flow, where static analysis results are fed into LLM agents (Abstraction, Details, Meta) to generate architectural insights and Mermaid.js diagrams.

components.name: Pipeline Orchestrator
components.description: Acts as the central control unit for the analysis lifecycle. It manages the execution context, validates configurations, and uses a planning agent to determine which parts of the codebase require expansion or deeper LLM-based reasoning.

components.name: Agent Intelligence Layer
components.description: Encapsulates the cognitive logic of the subsystem. It consists of specialized agents that perform high-level structural grouping (Abstraction) and implementation-level analysis (Details). These agents use repository tools to "sense" the codebase and interact with a caching layer to optimize LLM usage.

components.name: Analysis State Manager
components.description: Defines the "Unified Analysis" data model and handles the serialization of architectural insights. It ensures that the complex relationships and component hierarchies discovered by the agents are correctly persisted to disk and can be hydrated for incremental analysis runs.

components.name: Static Analysis Core

components.description: Acts as the central controller for the analysis process, determining whether to perform a full scan or an incremental update based on Git diffs. It manages the serialization of analysis results to disk to ensure fast subsequent runs.

components.name: Analysis Orchestrator
components.description: The central controller that manages the analysis lifecycle. It uses Git-based diffing to identify changed files and coordinates the execution of the analysis pipeline, deciding between full re-scans and incremental updates.

components.name: Graph Orchestrator
components.description: The central controller that manages the high-level workflow of the semantic analysis. It coordinates the discovery of symbols across the project and initiates the multi-pass process of building edges to construct the final call graph.

components.name: Semantic Resolver
components.description: Responsible for the complex logic of transforming raw LSP data into semantic relationships. it resolves references to their definitions, filters out non-callable symbols, and builds the inheritance tree to handle polymorphic call sites.

components.name: Symbol Registry
components.description: Acts as the "source of truth" for all discovered symbols. It maintains a global symbol table, handles qualified name generation across different languages, and provides canonical mapping for aliased symbols.

components.name: LSP & Language Adapter
components.description: The abstraction layer for external communication and language-specific nuances. It manages the lifecycle of Language Servers, handles JSON-RPC transport, and implements language-specific file discovery and configuration.

components.name: Architectural Clustering & Validation
components.description: Groups the low-level call graph into high-level architectural components using community detection algorithms. It also analyzes changes between versions and validates that the resulting clusters provide sufficient coverage of the codebase.

components.name: Clustering & Topology Engine
components.description: The core engine responsible for the structural transformation of the codebase. it applies community detection algorithms (Louvain/Leiden) to the call graph and synthesizes high-level relationships between the resulting clusters to define the system's architectural topology.

components.name: Incremental Evolution Manager
components.description: Manages the lifecycle of architectural analysis across different versions of the codebase. It prioritizes incremental updates by matching clusters between versions and calculating change metrics, significantly reducing LLM processing costs by only analyzing what has changed.

components.name: Validation & Coverage Guard
components.description: Acts as a quality assurance layer that verifies the integrity and completeness of the clustering process. It ensures that the generated architectural components provide sufficient coverage of the source code and that the naming and classification of components remain consistent.

components.name: Persistence & Cache Layer
components.description: Handles the serialization and management of analysis data. It ensures that call graphs and cluster results are efficiently stored in local databases (DuckDB) or file-based caches, enabling portability and fast retrieval for incremental analysis.

components_relations.relation: Orchestrates the clustering process by determining which parts of the graph require new community detection based on version diffs
Passes the synthesized architectural map and component relations for coverage and integrity verification

Supplies historical analysis state and cached graphs to enable version-to-version comparison and incremental updates

components.name: Change Analysis
components.description: Implements the incrementalism bias by detecting Git diffs and mapping changes to the existing analysis. It ensures that only modified code sections are re‑processed by the LLM agents.

components.name: Change Detection & Context Manager
components.description: Responsible for identifying raw changes in the repository and filtering them based on the project's environmental context. It maps line-level Git diffs to high-level code entities (like methods) and manages the persistence of previous analysis states to enable comparison.

components.name: Smart Analysis Orchestrator
components.description: The central coordinator that drives the "smart" update logic. It interfaces with the repository to detect changes and decides which parts of the static analysis graph need to be recalculated or sent to the LLM.

components.name: Change Detection Engine
components.description: Responsible for the granular comparison of code versions. It extracts Git diffs and performs structural analysis to determine if changes are functional (e.g., logic updates) or non-functional (e.g., comment changes), mapping these diffs to specific method boundaries.

components.name: Repository Context Manager
components.description: Manages the environmental boundaries of the analysis. It handles .gitignore-style exclusions via the RepoIgnoreManager and tracks which files have been successfully indexed or analyzed through the FileCoverage tracker.

components.name: Analysis Persistence Layer
components.description: Handles the serialization and retrieval of static analysis results (CFGs, call graphs, and entity metadata). It ensures that the "previous state" is available for the Change Detection Engine to compare against and manages path normalization for cross-environment cache validity.

components_relations.relation: delegates the identification of structural modifications to determine the scope of the current analysis run
queries the ignore manager and coverage tracker to filter out irrelevant files before initiating analysis
retrieves historical method hashes and structural metadata to perform granular diffing against the current live code

components.name: Incremental State Orchestrator
components.description: The logic engine that computes the delta between the previous analysis and the current changes. It updates the internal representation of the codebase (CFGs, dependencies, clusters) and performs validation to ensure the resulting analysis is consistent and grounded before it is passed to the agents.

components.name: Incremental Delta Engine
components.description: The primary logic engine for change detection. It compares the current state of the codebase against previous analysis snapshots to identify modified methods, classes, and files. It encapsulates the logic for computing IncrementalDelta objects and applying them to the global index to avoid redundant full-repo scans.

components.name: Analysis State & Validation
components.description: Acts as the "Source of Truth" for the subsystem. it manages the lifecycle of StaticAnalysisResults, including their persistence in the StaticAnalysisCache. This component also performs critical integrity checks, ensuring that LLM-generated clusters and abstractions remain grounded in the actual static analysis data (CFGs and dependencies).

components.name: LLM Context Provider
components.description: A serialization layer that translates internal machine-readable models (graphs, hierarchies, and deltas) into human-readable/LLM-optimized string formats. It implements the llm_str pattern across various data models to ensure that agents receive the most relevant and concise context for reasoning.

components_relations.relation: Computes and applies code changes to update the persistent analysis results and file indices.
Provides the validated, grounded codebase model to be serialized for agent consumption.
Supplies the previous analysis state required to compute the delta for the current iteration.

components.name: Analysis Serialization Layer
components.description: Defines the data contracts and serialization logic for communicating analysis results to LLM providers. It ensures that the complex internal state of the incremental analysis is formatted into the specific Pydantic models and schemas expected by the agentic framework.

components.name: Analysis Data Models
components.description: Defines the structural foundation and validation rules for analysis results using Pydantic. It ensures that complex code metadata—including components, relations, and method entries—is consistently represented and validated before being passed to the formatting layer.

components.name: LLM View Serializer
components.description: Implements the specialized serialization logic that converts structured data models into specific string formats required for LLM prompts. It handles the recursive rendering of components, relationships, and control flow graphs into a human-readable and LLM-optimized text format.

components.name: Agentic Orchestration & Execution

components.name: Reasoning & Validation Engine
components.description: Orchestrates the multi-stage analysis process, generating dynamic prompts and validating LLM outputs. It contains the specialized logic for clustering code components and refining architectural insights through self-correction.

components.name: Agent Execution & Tooling
components.description: Provides the operational runtime for agents, managing the lifecycle of LLM requests (retries, parsing) and providing a toolkit for repository exploration. It acts as the bridge between abstract reasoning and the concrete source code.

components.name: LLM Infrastructure & Observability
components.description: Manages LLM provider configurations (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.), model selection, and telemetry. It tracks token usage and execution flow to provide transparency into the agentic process.

components.name: Persistence & Incremental Cache
components.description: Implements the local-first storage strategy, caching LLM responses and analysis state to support incremental updates and minimize redundant API calls.

components.name: Structural Analysis & Health Engine
components.description: Performs algorithmic analysis on the codebase to create the "skeleton" for LLM reasoning. It uses graph-based clustering to group related code entities and runs diagnostic checks (like circular dependency detection) to provide objective health metrics.

components.name: Structural Graph & Clustering Engine
components.description: Constructs the directed call graph of the codebase and applies community detection algorithms to identify logical modules and clusters. It manages the underlying network structure and enforces cross-language budgets for cluster sizes.

components.name: Static Health Diagnostics
components.description: Executes automated checks to identify architectural smells, dead code, and dependency issues. It leverages LSP data to provide objective metrics and scoring, which are aggregated into a comprehensive health report.

components.name: Analysis Modeling & LLM Serialization
components.description: Translates raw graph data and health metrics into structured semantic representations optimized for LLM context windows. It handles the transformation of complex relationships into a format the agent can use for reasoning.

components.name: Prompt Factory & Provider Abstraction
components.description: Decouples the reasoning logic from specific LLM implementations. It manages complex prompt templates for different architectural tasks and translates them into provider-specific formats (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.), ensuring consistent behavior across different models.

components.name: Prompt Orchestration & Interface
components.description: Acts as the central gateway and contract definition for the subsystem. It defines the AbstractPromptFactory interface that all providers must implement and provides a singleton PromptFactory that dynamically selects and instantiates the correct provider implementation based on the active LLMType.

components.name: Provider-Specific Prompt Strategies
components.description: A suite of concrete implementations tailored to specific LLM backends. These classes encapsulate the "prompt engineering" logic, defining how system messages, planner instructions, and tool‑use feedback are formatted to optimize the performance of specific models like Claude, GPT‑4, or Gemini.

components_relations.relation: instantiates and delegates prompt generation requests to the active concrete strategy implementation

components.description: Acts as the "senses" and "memory" of the subsystem. It provides tools for agents to interact with the physical repository (reading files, checking diffs) and manages the local storage of analysis results to enable incremental updates.

components.name: Repository Access Layer
components.description: Acts as the primary interface for interacting with the physical file system and version control. It provides agents with the "senses" to discover files, read content, and identify changes via Git diffs.

components.name: Structural Analysis Engine
components.description: Responsible for the semantic interpretation of source code. It transforms raw file content into structured data models, resolving cross-file references and building relationship maps such as call graphs and class hierarchies.

components.name: Persistence & Caching Layer
components.description: Manages the long-term storage of analysis results and tool outputs. It handles path normalization (relativization) to ensure cache portability across different environments and manages SQLite connections for structured data retrieval.

components.name: Health & Documentation
components.description: Transforms internal analysis data into user‑facing outputs. It calculates architectural health metrics (coupling, cohesion) and generates final HTML/Markdown reports.

components.name: Health Metrics Evaluator
components.description: Calculates architectural quality metrics and identifies code smells by analyzing component relationships and internal structure, producing qualitative Findings.

components.name: Health Orchestration & Configuration
components.description: Manages the lifecycle of the health evaluation process, including environment setup, check execution, and result aggregation. It maintains the configuration schemas and data models that define finding structures and metric thresholds.

components.name: Relational Health Analyzers
components.description: Evaluates the connectivity and modularity of the system by analyzing relationships between components. This includes measuring fan‑in/fan‑out, component cohesion via call graph clustering, and identifying risks like circular dependencies or package instability.

components.name: Structural Health Analyzers
components.description: Focuses on the internal complexity and hierarchical structure of individual code entities. It identifies “God Classes” and oversized functions that violate the Single Responsibility Principle, and flags excessively deep inheritance trees.

components.name: External Diagnostic Bridge
components.description: Acts as an adapter for external quality signals, primarily from Language Server Protocol (LSP) providers. It collects and normalizes diagnostics such as unused code or deprecated API usage, integrating them into the unified health reporting framework.

components.name: Incremental Update Manager
components.description: Calculates deltas between repository states to ensure health metrics and documentation are updated only for changed components, adhering to the project’s incrementalism bias.

components.name: Orchestration & State Controller
components.description: Acts as the primary lifecycle manager for the incremental update process. It determines if a full re-analysis is required or if an incremental update is possible by comparing repository state hashes. It coordinates the flow between delta detection, graph synchronization, and final result merging.

components.name: Delta Computation Engine
components.description: Responsible for the low-level "diffing" logic. It compares two versions of the codebase to produce a structured IncrementalDelta, which categorizes changes into added, modified, or deleted files and methods. This precision allows the system to isolate the impact of code changes.

components.name: Structural Analysis & Clustering
components.description: Manages the logical partitioning of the code graph. When deltas are applied, this component re-runs community detection algorithms to adjust cluster boundaries, absorbs small code fragments into larger communities, and ensures that all modified methods are correctly mapped to architectural components.

components.name: Analysis Insight Formatter
components.description: Translates the internal structural data (clusters, relations, and component changes) into a format optimized for LLM reasoning. It implements the llm_str protocol across various response models to provide the agentic layer with a grounded, text-based view of the incremental changes.

components.name: CLI & Repository Interface
components.description: Entry point for the subsystem; handles environment setup, repository management, and filtering logic, orchestrating the start of the analysis pipeline.

components.name: CLI Orchestrator
components.description: The central controller that parses command-line arguments and manages the high-level execution logic. It determines whether to perform a local or remote analysis, handles incremental updates, and coordinates the sequence of operations from initialization to final output.

components.name: Environment & Tooling Manager
components.description: Responsible for setting up the infrastructure required for analysis. This includes loading user configurations, injecting API keys for LLM providers, and ensuring that external dependencies like language servers (LSP) are installed and available in the environment.

components.name: Repository & Workspace Manager
components.description: Manages the lifecycle of the source code being analyzed. It handles cloning remote repositories into temporary workspaces and applies filtering logic (via .gitignore and .codeboardingignore) to ensure the analysis engine only processes relevant files, optimizing both performance and LLM token usage.

components.name: Documentation & Output Finalizer
components.description: The final stage of the CLI pipeline. It consumes the structured analysis results (typically analysis.json) and invokes generators to produce the final Markdown onboarding materials. It also handles workspace cleanup and result persistence.

components_relations.relation: triggers environment setup and tool validation before any analysis logic begins
requests the preparation of the target source code, including cloning remote repos and identifying files to ignore
invokes the finalizer to generate user-facing documentation after core analysis pipeline completes
provides the filtered file list and local workspace paths required for the orchestrator to initialize the analysis context

components.name: Persistence & Tooling
components.description: Provides the underlying infrastructure, including local DuckDB persistence for analysis results, caching mechanisms, and the toolkit used by agents to interact with the filesystem.

components.name: Local Persistence Engine
components.description: Manages the physical storage of operational metadata and analysis results, implements content‑addressed caching using file signatures and model settings, and maintains a historical record of CLI tasks in a local DuckDB instance.

components.name: Base Caching Engine
components.description: Provides the foundational SQLite-backed storage and deterministic key generation logic. It implements the core content-addressed caching mechanism, using ModelSettings to ensure that cache entries are invalidated if LLM parameters (like temperature or model version) change.

components.name: Analysis Artifact Store
components.description: Implements specialized caches for high-level analysis data, including project metadata, code clusters, and final LLM insights. It extends the base caching logic to handle specific data structures and includes maintenance routines to prune stale analysis results and manage local storage growth.

components.name: Job History Ledger
components.description: Maintains a durable, structured record of CLI task executions using a local DuckDB instance. Unlike the content-addressed caches, this component tracks the operational "jobs"—individual runs of the tool—including their status, timing, and success rates for auditing and reporting.

components_relations.relation: Inherits storage logic and deterministic key generation to persist specialized analysis results.
Delegates low-level SQLite connection management and cache invalidation triggers.
Operates as a parallel persistence track, separating operational history (DuckDB) from analysis content (SQLite).

components.name: Repository Interaction Toolkit
components.description: Provides a tool‑use infrastructure that abstracts filesystem complexities for the agentic layer, maintaining repository context, ignore rules, and specialized tools for reading file contents and extracting external dependencies without exposing the raw filesystem to the LLM.

components.name: Repository Abstraction Layer
components.description: Manages the foundational state and filesystem abstraction, providing a consistent environment for all tools. It handles directory walking, caching, and the enforcement of ignore rules to ensure tools only interact with relevant project files.

components.name: File & Dependency Analysis Tools
components.description: Implements specialized tools for extracting source code, documentation, and external dependency information. These tools validate agent inputs via Pydantic models and perform targeted extraction of content and metadata.

components.name: Navigation & Discovery Tools
components.description: Provides high‑level visibility into the project's organization, allowing agents to explore the directory hierarchy efficiently without reading individual file contents.

components_relations.relation: inherits from BaseRepoTool
queries RepoContext and ignore_manager

components.name: Execution Monitoring & Telemetry
components.description: Tracks performance, cost, and lifecycle of agentic runs in real‑time via a callback‑based architecture that intercepts LLM and tool events, aggregates token usage and execution timing, and streams this data to the persistence layer for detailed run statistics and audit trails.

components.name: Lifecycle & Context Management
components.description: Orchestrates the activation and scope of monitoring sessions. It provides the entry points for instrumentation through context managers and decorators, ensuring that the telemetry infrastructure is correctly initialized and that execution metadata is captured at both the global run and granular function levels.

components.name: Telemetry Collection & Extraction
components.description: Acts as the event‑driven sensor layer of the subsystem. It implements callback interfaces to intercept lifecycle events from LLMs and tools. This component is responsible for parsing and normalizing raw provider responses (e.g., OpenAI vs. Anthropic usage formats) into a unified telemetry format.

components.name: Metrics Aggregation & Persistence
components.description: Manages the stateful accumulation of telemetry data and its serialization to storage. It aggregates raw events into structured statistics (token counts, total costs) and utilizes a background streaming writer to persist data to disk or external streams without blocking the agent's primary execution loop.
